summary: 18th-century Venetian Baroque/Rococo painter known for grand ceiling frescoes, especially at the Wurzburg Residence (Apollo and the Four Continents, world's largest ceiling fresco) and the Royal Palace of Madrid (Apotheosis of the Spanish Monarchy, Aeneas frescoes). Collaborated with quadratura specialist Girolamo Mengozzi-Colonna at the Palazzo Labia. Also decorated the Villa Valmarana. Son Giandomenico also a painter. Marcel Proust named a shade of pink after him.

works.name: Wurzburg Residence Frescoes (Apollo and the Four Continents)
works.description: Grand ceiling fresco at the Wurzburg Residence, designed by Balthasar Neumann. The world's largest ceiling fresco. Depicts Apollo touring the universe alongside personifications of Africa, America, Asia, and Europe. Includes Frederick Barbarossa's marriage in the Kaisersaal, where stucco putti pull back gilt stucco curtains to reveal the fresco. The main fresco decorates a huge trough vault over the Grand Staircase (Treppenhaus) and is gradually revealed as the viewer walks up.

clues.clue: Painted frescoes for the Wurzburg Residence, designed by Balthasar Neumann — his most famous commission

this 18th century Venetian painter who painted frescoes for the Wurzburg Residenz

works.name: Royal Palace of Madrid (Apotheosis of the Spanish Monarchy)
works.description: Frescoes at the Royal Palace of Madrid for Charles III, including The Apotheosis of the Spanish Monarchy and the Immaculate Conception. Painted Hercules alongside a column symbolizing Gibraltar and the Pillars of Hercules. Also painted Aeneas frescoes for the Guard Room ceiling, showing both his apotheosis and his presentation with arms forged by Vulcan.

works.name: Palazzo Labia (Cleopatra and Marc Antony series)
works.description: Frescoes at the Palazzo Labia in Venice, painted with quadratura specialist Girolamo Mengozzi-Colonna. Includes The Banquet of Cleopatra (Cleopatra dropping a pearl into vinegar) and Meeting of Marc Antony and Cleopatra. The frescoes occupy spaces between false pilasters and under false arches.

comprehensive_summary:
Giovanni Battista Tiepolo (1696-1770) was an 18th-century Venetian Baroque/Rococo painter, one of the last great Italian Old Master painters. He was known for his grand ceiling frescoes using di sotto in su and quadratura illusionistic techniques, and worked extensively in Venice, Germany (Wurzburg), and Spain (Madrid). His son Giandomenico also worked as a painter and often assisted him. He was a contemporary of Pietro Longhi and Canaletto in Venice.

Tiepolo's most famous work is the ceiling fresco cycle at the Wurzburg Residence in Bavaria, a palace designed by Balthasar Neumann. The main fresco, Apollo and the Four Continents (also called Allegory of the Planets and Continents), is the world's largest ceiling fresco, depicting Apollo touring the universe alongside personifications of Africa, America (a woman with a feathered headdress riding a crocodile), Asia, and Europe (seated on a pedestal beside a reclining portrait of Neumann). The fresco decorates a huge trough vault over the Grand Staircase and is gradually revealed as the viewer walks up the Treppenhaus (main staircase). The Kaisersaal in the same building contains a fresco of Frederick Barbarossa's marriage, revealed by stucco putti pulling back gilt stucco curtains.

At the Royal Palace of Madrid, Tiepolo worked for Charles III, painting The Apotheosis of the Spanish Monarchy (featuring the Pillars of Hercules symbolizing Gibraltar, Mercury crowning the allegory of royalty, and Ancient Castile personified as a tower on a cliffside), the Immaculate Conception (Virgin trampling a snake, dove and crown of stars), a ceiling fresco showing Neptune guiding a treasure-laden galleon, and an Aeneas fresco for the Guard Room ceiling showing both his apotheosis and his presentation with arms forged by Vulcan. His son Giandomenico assisted him there.

In Venice, Tiepolo collaborated with the quadratura specialist Girolamo Mengozzi-Colonna at the Palazzo Labia, painting The Banquet of Cleopatra (the queen drops a massive pearl into vinegar to win a wager about the most lavish feast, with a black dwarf in particolored outfit), the Meeting of Marc Antony and Cleopatra, and Bellerophon on Pegasus (with a gray obelisk symbolizing eternity). The frescoes occupy spaces between false pilasters and under false arches.

Other works include the Capricci and Scherzi print series (reimagining Castiglione and Salvator Rosa), Death Giving an Audience (skeletal dog), Punchinello drawings, Scipio Africanus Freeing Massiva, the Memento Mori, and Aeneas frescoes at the Villa Valmarana. Marcel Proust named a shade of pink after Tiepolo, referencing Odette Swann.
